title: Properties should be preferred
description: Properties should be preferredAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellconventionWhy is this an issue?Properties are accessed like fields which makes them easier to use.This rule raises an issue when the name of apublicorprotectedmethod starts withGet, takes no parameter, and returns a value that is not an array.Noncompliant code exampleusing System; namespace MyLibrary { public class Foo { private string name; public string GetName() // Noncompliant { return name; } } }Compliant solutionusing System; namespace MyLibrary { public class Foo { private string name; public string Name { get { return name; } } } }ExceptionsThe rule doesn’t raise an issue when the method:Is a constructorIs anoverrideIs an interface implementationIsasyncReturnsTask,Task<T>Is namedGetEnume
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.description: Detect getter/setter methods that should be properties.
Exclude override, async, and interface methods.
Exclude methods returning Task, Task<T>, or arrays.
